opts: Value of next best alternative foregone
et: Opportunity cost: value of the best alternative you give up when making a choice.
GDP: total value of all goods and services produced in a country in a year.
Inflation: sustained general increase in price levels over time. Measured by CPI.
SARB: controls monetary policy, sets repo rate, manages inflation target.

q: When demand increases and supply stays the same, price:
et: Demand↑ + Supply constant → Price↑. Basic supply and demand principle.

q: Unemployment rate in SA is approximately:
et: SA unemployment ≈33% (broad definition includes discouraged workers).
Monopoly: single seller with no close substitutes — dominates entire market.
Fiscal policy: government uses spending and taxation to influence the economy.

opts: Government spending leads to greater increase in GDP
et: Multiplier: initial spending creates rounds of further spending — GDP grows by more than initial injection.
Progressive tax: rate increases as income increases. SA personal income tax is progressive.
